use tree_sitter::Node;

const MAX_DEPTH: usize = 128;

fn walk_tree(node: Node, source: &str, depth: usize) {
    if depth > MAX_DEPTH {
        return;
    }
    let mut cursor = node.walk();
    for child in node.named_children(&mut cursor) {
        let text = &source[child.start_byte()..child.end_byte()];
        process_node(text);
        walk_tree(child, source, depth + 1);
    }
}

fn find_deepest(node: Node, depth: usize) -> usize {
    if depth > MAX_DEPTH {
        return depth;
    }
    let mut max_depth = depth;
    let mut cursor = node.walk();
    for child in node.named_children(&mut cursor) {
        let d = find_deepest(child, depth + 1);
        max_depth = max_depth.max(d);
    }
    max_depth
}

fn walk_iterative(root: Node, source: &str) {
    let mut stack: Vec<(Node, usize)> = vec![(root, 0)];
    while let Some((node, depth)) = stack.pop() {
        if depth > MAX_DEPTH {
            continue;
        }
        let mut cursor = node.walk();
        for child in node.named_children(&mut cursor) {
            let text = &source[child.start_byte()..child.end_byte()];
            process_node(text);
            stack.push((child, depth + 1));
        }
    }
}

fn process_node(_text: &str) {}
